questions.text: When the sum of the measures of two angles is 90°, the angles are called:
questions.explanation: Complementary angles sum up to exactly 90 degrees.

questions.text: When the sum of the measures of two angles is 180°, the angles are called:
questions.explanation: Supplementary angles sum up to exactly 180 degrees.

questions.text: Find the complement of 30°.
Find the supplement of 105°.
Two angles that have a common vertex and a common arm but no common interior points are:
questions.explanation: Adjacent angles are 'side-by-side' angles.

questions.text: A pair of adjacent angles whose non-common sides are opposite rays is called a:
questions.explanation: Linear pair angles always add up to 180°.

questions.text: When two lines intersect, the vertically opposite angles so formed are:
questions.explanation: Vertical angles are always equal in measure.

questions.text: If two lines are parallel and intersected by a transversal, the corresponding angles are:
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles occupy the same relative position at each intersection.

questions.text: The sum of interior angles on the same side of a transversal of two parallel lines is:
questions.explanation: Consecutive interior angles are supplementary.

questions.text: An angle which is equal to its complement is:
An angle which is equal to its supplement is:
If two angles are complementary, the sum of their measures is:
questions.explanation: Definition of complementary angles.

questions.text: If two angles are supplementary, the sum of their measures is:
questions.explanation: Definition of supplementary angles.

questions.text: Two right angles are always:
Which of the following cannot be a pair of complementary angles?
questions.explanation: Obtuse is already > 90, and two right angles sum to 180.

questions.text: In a linear pair, if one angle is acute, the other must be:
questions.explanation: To sum to 180, if one is < 90, the other must be > 90.

questions.text: If two lines intersect at a point, and if one pair of vertically opposite angles are acute angles, then the other pair of vertically opposite angles are:
questions.explanation: Adjacent angles in an intersection form a linear pair.

questions.text: A line that intersects two or more lines at distinct points is called a:
questions.explanation: Definition of a transversal.

questions.text: If a transversal intersects two parallel lines, each pair of alternate interior angles are:
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles form a 'Z' shape and are equal for parallel lines.

questions.text: The angles between two parallel lines on the same side of the transversal are:
questions.explanation: They sum to 180°.

questions.text: What is the measure of an angle that is 20° more than its complement?
How many transversals can be drawn for two given lines?
questions.explanation: Any line crossing them at distinct points is a transversal.

questions.text: Alternate exterior angles are:
questions.explanation: Standard property of parallel lines.

questions.text: If l || m and m || n, then:
questions.explanation: Lines parallel to the same line are parallel to each other.

questions.text: A line segment has ______ end points.
questions.explanation: A segment is part of a line with fixed start and end.

questions.text: A ray has ______ end point(s).
questions.explanation: A ray starts at a point and goes to infinity in one direction.

questions.text: When two lines intersect, they meet at ______ point(s).
questions.explanation: Distinct non-parallel lines intersect at exactly one point.

questions.text: An angle of 180° is called a:
questions.explanation: It looks like a straight line.

questions.text: If x + 50° forms a linear pair, then x is:
The measure of an angle which is four times its complement is:
Find the supplement of 0°.
Vertically opposite angles are always equal. This is true:
questions.explanation: It is a universal geometric theorem.

questions.text: The letter 'F' in geometry helps identify:
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles form an 'F' shape.

questions.text: The letter 'Z' in geometry helps identify:
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles form a 'Z' shape.

questions.text: Can two obtuse angles be supplementary?
questions.explanation: Two angles > 90 will sum to > 180.

questions.text: Can two acute angles be supplementary?
questions.explanation: Two angles < 90 will sum to < 180.

questions.text: If one angle of a linear pair is 90°, the other is:
Complement of 90° is:
Interior angles on the same side of the transversal are also called:
questions.explanation: These are synonymous terms in geometry.

questions.text: If a line is perpendicular to one of two parallel lines, it is ______ to the other.
questions.explanation: Standard property of parallel lines and transversals.
